title: 10 best herbal essential oils for swollen feet
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for swollen feet are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate swelling, pain, and discomfort in the feet. They work by reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal essential oils for swollen feet include Eucalyptus globulus, Rosmarinus officinalis, Cymbopogon citratus, Aloe barbadensis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Arnica montana, Melaleuca alternifolia, Lavandula angustifolia, and Hamamelis virginiana. If left untreated, swollen feet can cause mobility issues, chronic pain, and emotional distress, severely impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with swollen feet because of its natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. When applied topically, the oil's active compounds help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort by improving blood circulation and relaxing tense muscles. Additionally, lavender's soothing and calming effects can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can further contribute to alleviating swollen feet. By incorporating English lavender essential oils into your self-care routine, you can experience relief from swollen feet and enjoy a more comfortable day-to-day life.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: English Lavender Essential Oil has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent the growth of bacteria, fungi, and viruses on the skin, reducing the risk of infection and promoting a healthy environment for healing in swollen feet.

Anti-inflammatory: The essential oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and pain associated with swollen feet, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving discomfort and improving circulation.

Antioxidant: English Lavender Essential Oil is rich in antioxidants that help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy cell growth and reducing oxidative stress that can exacerbate swelling and discomfort in the feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen feet.
Buds: The buds of the English lavender plant are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after dew has dried off.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 35 degrees celsius for four hours.
Steam distill the dried lavandula angustifolia flowers using 20 grams of material per liter of water for 30 minutes.
Monitor and collect the essential oil that condenses in the condenser coil, yielding 5 milliliters of pure lavandula angustifolia essential oil.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities or contaminants within 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with swollen feet because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oil contains eucalyptol, which effectively reduces inflammation and relieves pain in the affected area. When applied topically to the swollen foot, the oil can help to reduce swelling by improving blood circulation and relaxing tense muscles. Additionally, the oil's decongestant properties can also help to alleviate discomfort and stiffness, promoting a sense of relief and comfort for those suffering from swollen feet.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with swollen feet by reducing inflammation.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of this essential oil can help prevent infection and promote healing of skin irritations and wounds caused by swollen feet, such as cracks and fissures.

Analgesic: The analgesic properties of Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil can provide relief from pain associated with swollen feet, such as aching and stiffness, by reducing discomfort and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with swollen feet.
Barks: The barks of the tree contain flavonoids, which have natural anti-swelling properties and are effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus leaves from mature trees in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the leaves in a warm oven at 60°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill 200g of dried eucalyptus leaves with 400ml of water for 4 hours.
Filter the essential oil mixture through a coffee filter paper and discard solids.
Store the resulting 30ml of eucalyptus globulus essential oil in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with swollen feet because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The oil's warming and soothing qualities also help to relax tense muscles and improve circulation, reducing fluid buildup in the feet. Additionally, curry plant essential oil has natural antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can help to prevent infections and promote healing. By massaging the oil into the affected area, individuals can experience relief from swollen and achy feet.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Curry Plant essential oil contains compounds like limonene, β-pinene, and γ-terpinene that reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, making it effective in reducing swelling in the feet.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich composition of Curry Plant essential oil helps neutralize free radicals that can contribute to foot swelling, promoting overall health and well-being in the lower extremities.

Analgesic and antispasmodic properties: Curry Plant essential oil contains analgesic compounds like sesquiterpenes and terpinen-4-ol, which help reduce pain and spasms associated with swollen feet, providing relief from discomfort and stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Curry roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them an effective ingredient in essential oils for relieving swelling and pain in the feet.

Barks: The barks of the curry plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helichrysum italicum flowers and leaves from the wild or cultivate them in your garden, ensuring at least 50 grams of plant material for processing.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it out in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, yielding approximately 30 grams of helichrysum italicum powder.

Combine 20 grams of helichrysum italicum powder with 100 ml of high-quality carrier oil such as sweet almond or jojoba oil in a clean glass jar, allowing the mixture to steep for 2 weeks at room temperature.

Filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and storing the resulting essential oil in an amber glass bottle protected from light and heat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium essential oils helps with swollen feet because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The oil's ability to promote blood circulation also helps to relieve congestion and alleviate pain associated with swollen feet. Additionally, wild geranium essential oils have natural astringent and antiseptic qualities, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation caused by swelling.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of wild geranium essential oil help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to chronic swelling and pain in the feet.

Vasodilatory: Wild geranium essential oil helps to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing fluid buildup in the tissues, which can help to alleviate swelling and discomfort in the feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with foot conditions.
Seeds: The seeds contain fatty acids and antioxidants that aid in soothing and calming irritated skin, providing relief from swelling and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves and stems of geranium maculatum flowers and foliage in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the plant material using a dehydrator at 30 degrees celsius for six hours or air drying for seven days.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a steam distillation apparatus with a yield of 0.5% essential oil.
Filter the resulting distillate through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and sediment within an hour.
Store the extracted geranium maculatum essential oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with swollen feet because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The oil's soothing and calming effects also help to relax tense muscles and improve blood circulation, further alleviating the discomfort associated with swollen feet. Antioxidant properties: Chamomile essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to swelling and discoloration in the feet, promoting overall foot health.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with swollen feet.
Flowers: Containing apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing swelling and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Cleanse the harvested flowers by gently stirring them in water and straining through cheesecloth.
Steam distill the cleansed flowers for 30 minutes at a pressure of 1 atmosphere using a copper alembic still.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the aqueous phase after allowing it to settle for 24 hours.
Dilute the collected essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a ratio of 2-3% matricaria chamomilla essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with swollen feet because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. When massaged into the skin, it can help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by fatigue, poor circulation, or injury. The antiseptic properties of thyme oil also make it an effective treatment for fungal infections that can cause foot fungus and odor. remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Thyme essential oil's antiseptic property helps to prevent infection and reduce inflammation in the feet, which can contribute to swelling.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory property of thyme essential oil reduces pain and swelling by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation.
Decongestant: Thyme essential oil's decongestant property helps to reduce excess fluid retention in the feet, which can contribute to swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease swelling, making them a popular choice for relieving swollen feet.
Leaves: Thyme leaves are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can be extracted into an essential oil to soothe and calm swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers, ensuring they are free of contaminants and pesticides.
Dry the harvested herbs using a food dehydrator at 70°f for 24 hours or air drying method for 3-4 weeks.
Steam distill the dried herbs in a dedicated essential oil still with a ratio of 1:3 herbs to water, monitoring temperature at 140°f for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting distillate through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment, then store the clear liquid in dark glass bottles.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 48 hours before use or storage, which enables optimal chemical composition and fragrance development.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves by cutting off sprigs from healthy plants early morning.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 35 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a ceramic still with water at a ratio of 10:1 for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the obtained essential oil through a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle.
Store the extracted essential oil in an airtight container away from direct sunlight for 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with swollen feet because of its exceptional anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh sclarea leaves and rinse them with cold distilled water to remove impurities.
Steam distill the sclarea leaves with 3000ml of distilled water for 3 hours to extract their aromatic compounds.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a clean glass container, filtering it through a paper coffee filter to remove any impurities.
Add 5 drops of sweet orange essential oil and mix well to create a soothing blend promoting circulation and reducing swelling.
Store the herbal salvia sclarea essential oil blend in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to 6 months, shaking gently before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense essential oils helps with swollen feet because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, frankincense oil can reduce swelling and ease pain caused by conditions such as plantar fasciitis or overexertion. The oil's ability to stimulate circulation also helps to flush out toxins and promote healthy tissue growth, further alleviating discomfort and promoting relaxation in the feet. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh boswellia serrata leaves and flowers in the early morning or late evening when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested botanicals using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or until crispy dryness is achieved.
Mix one tablespoon of dried boswellia serrata with two cups of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil and stir well.
Place the mixture in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks to allow for infusion.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids then transfer the infused oil into airtight containers.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helichrysum stoechas flowers in the early morning for maximum oil content retention.
Dry the harvested flowers using air circulation at room temperature for two weeks, or until brittle and crumbly.
Blend 1 cup of dried helichrysum stoechas flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil, such as sweet almond or jojoba oil, in a clean glass jar.
Allow the blend to macerate in a cool dark place for three weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining and discarding solids.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a small glass bottle, discarding any remaining sediment, and store at room temperature for up to six months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat swollen feet with herbal essential oils is to massage them gently onto the affected area using a combination of peppermint, eucalyptus, and chamomile oils. These oils help reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and soothe tired feet. Mix equal parts of each oil with a carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil, apply a small amount to your feet, and massage in circular motions for 5-10 minutes before rinsing off with warm water.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen feet that are treated with herbal essential oils are conditions like varicose veins, cellulite, and water retention. Essential oils like peppermint, eucalyptus, and grapefruit help to improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote lymphatic drainage, which can alleviate symptoms of these conditions. Additionally, oils like lavender and chamomile can also help to soothe and calm the affected area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with swollen feet because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds such as eucalyptol, which helps reduce swelling and ease pain. It also has decongestant properties that promote blood circulation, which can help to reduce fluid retention in the feet. Additionally, the oil's cooling sensation can help to soothe and calm the skin, providing relief from discomfort and tension. This makes it an effective natural remedy for swollen feet. Regular use can bring welcome relief.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with swollen feet.
α-Phellandrene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the feet.
β-Caryophyllene: Demonstrates analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to alleviate pain and reduce swelling in swollen feet.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the most commonly used part of Eucalyptus globulus for essential oil production, as they are rich in eucalyptol, a primary compound responsible for the oil's therapeutic properties.
Barks: The barks of Eucalyptus globulus are used to make essential oils, particularly for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the feet.

Stems: The stems of Eucalyptus globulus are also used to produce essential oils, often in combination with leaves or barks, to take advantage of their medicinal properties, including decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves on a sunny day to ensure optimal oil content.
Dry the leaves in a warm place for 2 days to reduce moisture and prevent mold growth.
Steam distill the dried leaves at 100c for 3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and improve its clarity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with swollen feet because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing swelling and discomfort. The oil's natural decongestant properties also help to improve circulation, allowing for efficient removal of excess fluids from the body. Additionally, rosmarinus officinalis essential oils has a stimulating effect on lymphatic circulation, which plays a crucial role in reducing swelling and promoting overall foot health, providing relief from swollen and tired feet.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene oxide: Acts as a potent anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain associated with swollen feet.
Bornyl acetate: Exhibits analgesic properties, providing relief from pain caused by inflammation and swelling in the feet.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation in the feet.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling.
Stems: The stems of Rosmarinus officinalis are used to create essential oils that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting circulation, which can help alleviate swollen feet.
Flowers: The flowers of the plant are used to create essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe and calm swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves by cutting them in the morning when the plant is at its peak potency.
Steam distill the fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves for 2-3 hours in a ratio of 1 part leaves to 2 parts water.
Collect the distilled rosmarinus officinalis essential oil and separate it from the water and other impurities using a separating funnel.
Filter the collected rosmarinus officinalis essential oil through a filter paper to remove any remaining impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with swollen feet because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The oil, commonly known as lemongrass oil, contains compounds that reduce inflammation and ease pain. It also has a warming effect that promotes blood circulation, helping to reduce swelling and discomfort. Additionally, lemongrass oil's decongestant properties help to eliminate excess fluids and toxins, further contributing to its effectiveness in alleviating swollen feet. remedy_constituents: Citral: This constituent helps reduce inflammation and swelling in feet due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Limonene: Limonene aids in reducing swelling by improving lymphatic circulation and promoting the elimination of excess fluids from the affected area.
Geraniol: Geraniol helps to relax muscles and improve blood circulation, contributing to reduced swelling and pain in swollen feet.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cymbopogon citratus are the most used part to make essential oils for swollen feet, as they contain high levels of citral and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Cymbopogon citratus are another key component, as they contain citral and other compounds that help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation associated with swollen feet.

Stems: The stems of Cymbopogon citratus are also used to make essential oils, as they contain a combination of citral, geraniol, and other compounds that help to promote relaxation and reduce swelling in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stems of cymbopogon citratus by cutting them at a 45-degree angle just above the node.
Steam distill the harvested stems at a temperature of 100-110 degrees celsius for 3-4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a clean glass container using a separating funnel.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at a temperature of 15-20 degrees celsius.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils helps with swollen feet because it possesses anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The oil's cooling effect also helps to relax tense muscles and calm the skin, promoting blood flow and relaxation. Additionally, its moisturizing properties help to soften and hydrate the skin, reducing the appearance of swelling and leaving the feet feeling refreshed and rejuvenated. remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Helps reduce swelling by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes and suppressing the production of inflammatory cytokines.
Glucomannan: Helps reduce swelling by absorbing excess fluid and improving lymphatic circulation, thereby reducing water retention in the feet.
Aloe vera glycoproteins: Helps reduce swelling by modulating the immune response, reducing inflammation, and improving skin elasticity and firmness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe barbadensis contain a high concentration of aloe vera gel, which is used to create essential oils that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in swollen feet.
Gel from Leaves (inner part of leaves): The inner gel of Aloe barbadensis leaves is rich in anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds that help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the feet.
Gel from Leaves (outer part of leaves): The outer gel of Aloe barbadensis leaves contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature aloe barbadensis leaves with thick green skin and no signs of decay or damage.
Clean and dry the aloe leaves in a cool and well-ventilated area for 24 hours.
Crush the dried aloe leaves into a fine pulp using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Steam distill the aloe pulp using a distillation apparatus at 100°c for 2 hours.
Collect the resulting aloe essential oil and store it in a cool dark place for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with swollen feet because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Help reduce inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to swollen feet.

Licoricidin: Display anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties that help alleviate swelling and discomfort associated with swollen feet by neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress.
Flavonoids: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects that help improve blood circulation, reduce swelling, and ease pain in swollen feet by promoting the relaxation of blood vessels.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and relieve pain in swollen feet.

Barks: They contain a combination of compounds that help reduce swelling, ease pain, and improve circulation in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matured glycyrrhiza glabra roots with a minimum of 2-3 years of age for high-quality essential oil.
Clean the harvested roots thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the cleaned roots at a temperature range of 100-110°c for 2-3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation mixture using a separator or a centrifuge.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh roots of zingiber officinale in the early morning or late evening when the plant is dormant.
Wash the harvested roots with cold water to remove dirt and debris then dry with a clean towel.
Steam distill the dried roots at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours using a steam distillation apparatus.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separating funnel and filter the oil through a coffee filter.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and keep it in a cool dry place.

Flavonoids: These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation in the feet, promoting overall healing and recovery.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are primarily used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and swelling in swollen feet.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the arnica montana flowers during the full bloom stage for optimal oil production.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers to remove any dirt or moisture.
Steam distill the dried arnica montana flowers at 100 to 150 mmhg pressure for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil from the distillation apparatus and store in a dark glass bottle.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities before storing for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with swollen feet because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The oil's natural decongestant properties also help to improve circulation, allowing the body to more efficiently remove excess fluids from the affected area. Additionally, Melaleuca alternifolia's antimicrobial properties can help to prevent infection, promoting a healthy recovery process. By incorporating this oil into a foot care routine, individuals can experience relief from swollen feet and promote overall foot health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in swollen feet due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Limonene: Exhibits diuretic properties, which aid in reducing fluid retention and swelling in the feet.
Triptolide: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe and heal skin irritations associated with swollen feet.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of cineole, which has anti-inflammatory properties to reduce swelling.
Buds: They are used due to their high content of cineole and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in swollen feet.
Stems: They are used due to their ability to provide a rich source of essential oils with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves from the plant's terminal branches at the peak of their potency.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at a temperature range of 70-100°c for 2-3 hours to release their essential oil.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separator or a centrifuge within 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter paper to remove any impurities or contaminants that may be present.
Store the final product in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at a temperature range of 15-30°c to preserve its potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with swollen feet because of its anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which helps to alleviate swelling and discomfort. Additionally, the oil's soothing and relaxing effects can help to reduce stress and promote a sense of calm, which can also contribute to the reduction of swelling and discomfort associated with swollen feet. This can lead to improved comfort and mobility.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the feet due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Linalyl acetate: Contributes to pain relief and reduction of swelling in the feet by modulating the nervous system and reducing pain transmission.
Caryophyllene oxide: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate swelling and discomfort in swollen feet.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make essential oils due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Lavandula angustifolia are primarily used to make essential oils as they possess a calming effect and are rich in antioxidants.
Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia are also used to make essential oils, although to a lesser extent, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the harvested flowers in a distillation apparatus at 100°c for 2-3 hours to separate oil from water.
Separate the essential oil from the water mixture using a separation funnel and collect the oil in a glass container.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and improve its quality.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its aroma.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana essential oils helps with swollen feet because it is a natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent. Its active compounds, such as tannins and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can alleviate swelling and discomfort. The oil's astringent properties also aid in reducing fluid retention and promoting healthy blood flow, making it an effective remedy for swollen feet. Regular use of Hamamelis virginiana essential oils can provide long-lasting relief and promote overall foot health.
remedy_constituents: Hamamelitannin: This tannin helps reduce inflammation and ease swelling in the feet by constricting blood vessels and limiting the release of histamine.
Hamamelin: This phenolic compound has astringent properties, which help reduce swelling and ease pain in the feet by contracting blood vessels and tightening tissues.
Oleanolic acid: This triterpenoid has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease pain in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their rich content of witch hazel, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and soothe aching feet.
Barks: The barks are used for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and promote circulation in the feet.
Roots: The roots are used for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to relieve pain and reduce swelling in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the leaves and twigs of hamamelis virginiana at dawn when the dew is still present for 2 hours.
Steam distill 100 grams of the harvested plant material with 200 milliliters of water for 3 hours at 100 degrees celsius.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distillate using a condenser and a separator for 1 hour.
Filter the collected essential oil through a microfilter or a coffee filter to remove impurities for 30 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place for 6 months.
